body.title: DickSimnel toolloop.py: add method docstrings
body.intention: I intend that every public and private method in toolloop.py has a one-line docstring so readers understand each tool's contract without reading the implementation.
body.description:
devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py implements the four inference tool stubs (_tool_bash, _tool_read, _tool_edit, _tool_write) plus __init__. All five methods have no docstring — the what-and-why is invisible to a reader. This is a pure docstring pass.

**Affected files:** devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code; one short line per method (no multi-paragraph blocks)
**Scope boundary:** In scope: add one-line docstrings to __init__, _tool_bash, _tool_read, _tool_edit, _tool_write. Out of scope: changing method signatures, adding type hints, touching other files.
**Completion criteria:** All 5 methods have a docstring. `python3 -c "import ast; t=ast.parse(open('devices/dicksimnel/toolloop.py').read()); funcs=[n for n in ast.walk(t) if isinstance(n,(ast.FunctionDef,ast.AsyncFunctionDef))]; assert all(n.body and isinstance(n.body[0],ast.Expr) for n in funcs), 'missing docstring'"` passes.
